steps.title: Find the constant c using function p
steps.content:
Since $p$ has slope 7, we can write $p(x) = 7x + b$. Using $p(3) = 23$: $$23 = 7(3) + b = 21 + b$$ Thus $b = 2$, so $p(x) = 7x + 2$.
Now use $p(c) = -5$: $$-5 = 7c + 2$$ $$7c = -7$$ $$c = -1$$

steps.title: Calculate the slope of t using two points
steps.content:
We now know $c = -1$. Function $t$ passes through the points $(-1, -6)$ and $(4, 34)$ (since $t(c) = t(-1) = -6$ and $t(4) = 34$).
Using the slope formula: $$\text{slope} = \frac{34 - (-6)}{4 - (-1)} = \frac{40}{5} = 8$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
